goal: Enforce 95% soft limits on IRB 6700 joints via a motion-planner-side trim (not articulation attrs) and shape trajectory jerk via Lula/RMPflow or a vel/accel profile — because URDF joint limits are hard.
thoughts: URDF joint limits in the articulation are HARD limits — PhysX clamps on violation. Soft limits at 95% belong in the motion planner or trajectory trimmer, not the articulation. set_joint_targets emits step inputs; drive P/D + maxForce shape the RESPONSE but give you no jerk control. ABB-style trapezoidal vel/accel/jerk profile needs Lula RMPflow or a scripted spline. RAPID is proprietary — can't import; JSON config the engineer maintains is the honest path. ISO 10218-1 >=5% soft-limit margin standard.
code:
# 1. Inspect existing drive APIs and joint limits on joint 2
list_all_prims(root="/World/IRB_6700", schema_filter="PhysicsDriveAPI")

# 2. Confirm ABB rated vel/accel spec before tuning drives
lookup_product_spec(query="ABB IRB 6700 235/2.65 joint rated velocity acceleration specification manual")

# 3. Motion planner patterns (Lula / RMPflow / Cortex) that DO give jerk control
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim Lula RMPflow Cortex motion generator trapezoidal velocity jerk limit")

# 4. Drive tuning for max vel / max force (shapes response, not jerk directly)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/IRB_6700/joint_2",
    attribute="drive:angular:physics:maxVelocity",
    value="<MAX_VEL_JOINT2>"   # match ABB rated spec
)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/IRB_6700/joint_2",
    attribute="drive:angular:physics:maxForce",
    value="<MAX_FORCE_JOINT2>"
)

# 5. Do NOT change hard joint limits -- keep URDF authority, trim at planner level:
#    SOFT_LIMITS = {'joint_2': (-65*0.95, 85*0.95), ...}  # JSON maintained by engineer
#    targets = clamp(targets, SOFT_LIMITS)  before issuing to articulation

# 6. If the engineer explicitly wants to expose soft limits in USD for visualization
#    (optional, not replacing the planner trim):
set_joint_limits(
    prim_path="/World/IRB_6700/joint_2",
    lower="<SOFT_LO_DEG>",
    upper="<SOFT_HI_DEG>"
)
failure_modes: Setting USD joint limits to 95% and expecting the planner to obey — planner reads URDF source
Just lowering max velocity and calling it jerk ramping — gives you vel control, not jerk
Promising a RAPID-import path — ABB RAPID is proprietary, no importer
Forgetting ISO 10218-1 >=5% margin — soft limits at 99% violate the standard
